messages.author: that’s_a_UNIX_book
messages.text: Just gettin started in Tucson area - see a few routers on 908
messages.content_html: Just gettin started in Tucson area - see a few routers on 908
messages.reactions_html: 3

messages.author: Sonny AA7K
messages.text: Just moved a couple of my devices (Wio Tracker L1 Pro and Wis Block (4631)) onto 'core from 'tastic. I am up in Queen Creek, and despite seeing a number of node placements on the map, I have seen ZERO actual traffic or devices online. I'm in the process of building a solar repeater around the Wis Block and should be able to get it up around 30 ft. AGL. If anyone is in the SE Phoenix Valley, I'd love to join forces. I have not been happy with the usability on the 'tastic side and what I've read/heard about Core sounds appealing. I'll probably stay at it for a while. I don't mind being in early. I'm also a Ham (AA7K). I'll be at the Scottsdale Springfest next Saturday if anyone else is around. I'll be wearing a black cap with gold emroidery of my call sign. If you see me, say hello

messages.author: that’s_a_UNIX_book
messages.text: Are you using the correct radio settings?
messages.content_html: Are you using the correct radio settings?

messages.author: Sonny AA7K
messages.text: @that’s_a_UNIX_book sorry I didn't see your reply. All good - I discovered the correct settings and am receiving a nice assortment of repeaters. Haven't made any text connections yet but hope to while wandering in Scottsdale on Saturday.
